name: Green Garlic Chili
recipeIngredient: salt and white pepper to taste
6 whole to 8 fresh New Mexico green chilies or 2 to 4 small ones
3 large fresh, home-grown green tomatoes (store-bought will do in a pinch as long as they are green)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a heavy Dutch oven or stainless pot.
2. Cook pork until it is white and browned on all sides. Remove the pork from the pot.
3. Sauté garlic and onion in the oil until the onions are clear.
4. Chop tomatoes and green chilies; add them to the onion-garlic mixture.
5. Return the pork to the pot. Add enough water to cover the ingredients, then simmer, covered, for about 1½ to 2 hours until the pork is done and the chili sauce is thick and rich looking.
6. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.
7. Serve the chili alone or in flour tortillas with plenty of picante sauce.
